title: The Bloomberg Terminal Is Getting an AI Makeover, Like It or Not
description: WIRED spoke with Bloomberg's chief technology officer about the big, chatbot-style changes coming to the iconic platform for traders.

title: Some Musk v. Altman Jurors Don't Like Elon Musk
description: Musk’s lawsuit challenges OpenAI’s evolution under Sam Altman. But during jury selection, several potential jurors voiced negative views of Musk himself.

title: Put it in pencil: NASA's Artemis III mission will launch no earlier than late 2027
description: SpaceX and Blue Origin tell NASA their lunar landers will be ready for Artemis III in late 2027.

title: Elon Musk and Sam Altman are going to court over OpenAI’s future
description: After a yearslong legal feud, Elon Musk and OpenAI CEO Sam Altman are heading to trial this week in Northern California in a case that could have sweeping consequences. Ahead of OpenAI’s highly anticipated IPO, the court could rule on whether the company is allowed to exist as a for-profit enterpris

title: Open source package with 1 million monthly downloads stole user credentials
description: If you're one of millions using element-data, it's time to check for compromise.

title: Musk and Altman face off in trial that will determine OpenAI's future
description: Musk’s shifting stance on AI dangers may complicate trial over OpenAI’s mission.

title: OpenAI ends its exclusive partnership with Microsoft
description: Amended agreement clears the way for OpenAI models to run on Amazon Bedrock.

title: Elon Musk Boosts New Yorker’s Sam Altman Exposé on X as Trial Begins
description: The move comes as the trial for Elon Musk’s lawsuit against OpenAI kicks off in federal court in Oakland.

title: EU tells Google to open up AI on Android; Google says that's "unwarranted intervention"
description: Gemini gets preferential treatment on Android, but maybe not for long (in Europe).

title: OpenAI ends Microsoft legal peril over its $50B Amazon deal
description: OpenAI has won major concessions from its largest shareholder, Microsoft, that will allow it to sell products on AWS, while Microsoft gets more cash in a revenue-share agreement.

title: DeepMind’s David Silver just raised $1.1B to build an AI that learns without human data
description: Ineffable Intelligence, a British AI lab founded a mere few months ago by former DeepMind researcher David Silver, has raised $1.1 billion in funding at a valuation of $5.1 billion.

title: The missing step between hype and profit
description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. title: Investors back Skye’s AI home screen app for iPhone ahead of launch
description: Skye's new AI app attracted investors before it even launched — a sign of interest in a more AI-aware iPhone.

title: The Man Behind AlphaGo Thinks AI Is Taking the Wrong Path
description: David Silver has a new billion-dollar company that aims to build AI “superlearners.”

title: China blocks Meta’s $2B Manus deal after months-long probe
description: China has ordered Meta to unwind its multibillion-dollar Manus acquisition, dealing a potential setback to Zuckerberg’s push into AI agents.

title: OpenAI could be making a phone with AI agents replacing apps
description: There have been plenty of rumors about OpenAI's hardware plans, which involve launching a pair of earbuds. A new note from industry analyst Ming-Chi Kuo suggests that the AI company might be working on a phone in collaboration with MediaTek, Qualcomm, and Luxshare.

title: Rebuilding the data stack for AI
description: Artificial intelligence may be dominating boardroom agendas, but many enterprises are discovering that the biggest obstacle to meaningful adoption is the state of their data. description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. title: Discord Sleuths Gained Unauthorized Access to Anthropic’s Mythos
description: Plus: Spy firms tap into a global telecom weakness to track targets, 500,000 UK health records go up for sale on Alibaba, Apple patches a revealing notification bug, and more.

title: Three reasons why DeepSeek’s new model matters
description: On April 24, Chinese AI firm DeepSeek released a preview of V4, its long-awaited new flagship model. The model can process much longer prompts than its last generation, thanks to a new design that helps it handle large amounts of text more efficiently. title: Understanding Convolutions on Graphs
description: Understanding the building blocks and design choices of graph neural networks.

title: A Gentle Introduction to Graph Neural Networks
description: What components are needed for building learning algorithms that leverage the structure and properties of graphs?

title: Distill Hiatus
description: After five years, Distill will be taking a break.

title: Adversarial Reprogramming of Neural Cellular Automata
description: Reprogramming Neural CA to exhibit novel behaviour, using adversarial attacks.

title: Weight Banding
description: Weights in the final layer of common visual models appear as horizontal bands. We investigate how and why.
